prompt: How can I apply for a personal loan?
chosen: You can apply for a personal loan online through the bank's website or mobile app, or by visiting a branch. You will need to submit your identity proof, address proof, salary slips, income tax returns, and bank statements for the last 3-6 months. The bank will assess your credit score, income, and repayment capacity before approving the loan.
rejected: Just go to the bank and ask for money.

prompt: What should I do if I receive a suspicious OTP request from someone claiming to be from my bank?
chosen: Never share your OTP with anyone, even if they claim to be a bank employee. Banks never ask for OTP over the phone or email. Hang up immediately, block the number, and report the incident to your bank's fraud helpline and the cybercrime portal at cybercrime.gov.in. Your OTP is the last line of security for your account.
rejected: Share the OTP if the person sounds genuine and official.

prompt: My EMI payment failed due to insufficient balance. What should I do?
chosen: Ensure sufficient funds in your account immediately and contact the bank to initiate the missed EMI payment. Most banks allow you to pay the missed EMI along with a small bounce charge. Avoid repeated defaults as they negatively impact your CIBIL score and may attract penal interest. Set up auto-debit or standing instructions with adequate balance.
rejected: Don't worry about it. Banks don't care about missed payments.

prompt: How do I transfer money using NEFT?
chosen: Log in to your bank's net banking portal or mobile app. Go to 'Fund Transfer' and select 'NEFT'. Add the beneficiary's name, account number, bank name, and IFSC code. Enter the amount and remarks, verify the OTP sent to your mobile, and confirm the transfer. NEFT transactions are processed in batches and complete within a few hours.
rejected: You can't transfer money online; you have to go to the bank.

prompt: What is the best way to improve my credit score?
chosen: Pay all loan EMIs and credit card bills on or before the due date consistently. Keep your credit utilization ratio below 30%. Avoid applying for multiple loans in a short period. Maintain a good mix of secured and unsecured credit. Check your credit report regularly for errors and dispute inaccuracies. Building a strong repayment history over time is the most effective way to improve your score.
rejected: Credit scores are not important. Just ignore them.

prompt: Can I withdraw cash from an ATM abroad using my debit card?
chosen: Yes, you can withdraw cash from international ATMs using your debit card if international usage is enabled. Enable the international use feature through your bank's mobile app or net banking before travelling. Transaction charges and currency conversion fees apply. Daily withdrawal limits may be lower than domestic limits. Inform your bank about your travel dates to avoid card blocks.
rejected: Debit cards don't work abroad.

prompt: What happens if I don't pay my credit card bill by the due date?
chosen: If you miss the payment due date, you will be charged a late payment fee and interest on the outstanding balance at your card's annual rate (typically 36-42%). Your credit score may also be negatively impacted. To minimize damage, pay at least the minimum amount due by the due date and pay the full amount as soon as possible to stop interest from compounding.
rejected: Nothing will happen. Banks are flexible about due dates.

prompt: How do I reactivate a dormant bank account?
chosen: To reactivate a dormant account, visit your bank branch with your passbook, debit card, and updated KYC documents (Aadhaar, PAN, address proof). Submit a written reactivation request. Alternatively, some banks allow reactivation through net banking or mobile banking with OTP verification. The bank will verify your identity and restore full account functionality.
rejected: You cannot reactivate a dormant account. You need to open a new one.

prompt: Is it safe to do net banking on public Wi-Fi?
chosen: Avoid conducting net banking transactions on public Wi-Fi networks as they are vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks and data theft. If necessary, use a VPN for an encrypted connection. Always verify the bank's website URL starts with HTTPS and shows a padlock icon. It is safest to use your personal mobile data for sensitive banking transactions.
rejected: Public Wi-Fi is perfectly safe for banking. Use it freely.

prompt: How can I reduce my home loan EMI?
chosen: You can reduce your home loan EMI by: making partial prepayments to reduce the outstanding principal, opting to extend the loan tenure (which reduces EMI but increases total interest), refinancing to a lower interest rate with another bank through balance transfer, or requesting your bank to recalculate the EMI at a lower rate if interest rates have dropped. Each option has trade-offs.
rejected: You cannot change your home loan EMI once fixed.

prompt: What is the purpose of Form 15G or 15H?
chosen: Form 15G (for individuals below 60 years) and Form 15H (for senior citizens) are self-declaration forms submitted to the bank to prevent TDS deduction on interest income if your total income is below the taxable limit. Submit these forms at the beginning of each financial year for all accounts and FDs across all banks where you earn interest.
rejected: Form 15G and 15H have no use. TDS is always deducted on FD interest.

prompt: What should I do before taking a loan to ensure I get the best deal?
chosen: Before taking a loan, check your credit score and address any issues. Compare interest rates (APR, not just nominal rates), processing fees, prepayment charges, and other terms across multiple lenders. Use EMI calculators to understand your monthly payment. Read all loan agreement terms carefully. A higher credit score and good income documentation can help negotiate better rates.
rejected: Just take the first loan offered to you. All banks offer the same rates.

prompt: How long does it take to process a RTGS transfer?
chosen: RTGS transfers are settled in real time on a gross basis, meaning the amount is credited to the beneficiary's account within 30 minutes, often within seconds during working hours. RTGS is available from 7 AM to 6 PM on weekdays and 7 AM to 1 PM on Saturdays. Outside these hours, transactions may be queued for the next working session.
rejected: RTGS takes 3-5 business days to process.

prompt: What is the consequence of closing a bank account before 12 months?
chosen: Most banks charge an early closure fee if you close a savings account within 12 months of opening, typically Rs. 300 to Rs. 500. No charges apply after 12 months. Salary accounts and zero-balance accounts may have different policies. Before closing, ensure all standing instructions, auto-debits, and linked services are cancelled to avoid complications.
rejected: You can close a bank account whenever you want with no consequences.

prompt: What documents are needed for a home loan application?
chosen: For a home loan you need: identity proof (Aadhaar/PAN/passport), address proof, income documents (salary slips for 3-6 months, Form 16, ITR for 2-3 years), bank statements for 6-12 months, property documents (sale agreement, NOC, property registration papers), and employer/business proof. Self-employed applicants also need business financials.
rejected: You only need an Aadhaar card to apply for a home loan.

prompt: Can I open a savings account online without visiting a branch?
chosen: Yes, many banks offer video KYC (V-CIP) based account opening online. You can apply through the bank's website or app, fill in your details, upload documents digitally, and complete identity verification via a live video call with a bank representative. An Aadhaar-based OTP verification may also be used. A debit card and cheque book are delivered to your address.
rejected: You must visit a branch in person to open any bank account.

prompt: What should I check before signing a loan agreement?
chosen: Before signing, verify: the exact interest rate and type (fixed/floating), all fees (processing, prepayment, late payment), the loan tenure, EMI amount and schedule, conditions for rate revision (for floating loans), consequences of default, and foreclosure terms. Ask the bank to explain any unclear terms. Never sign blank forms and keep copies of all documents.
rejected: Just sign wherever the banker tells you. The terms are always standard.

prompt: My credit card was charged for something I didn't buy. What should I do?
chosen: Immediately call your bank's 24/7 customer care to block your credit card and report the unauthorized transaction. Request a chargeback by raising a dispute through the mobile app, net banking, or a branch. Submit a dispute form within the specified time (typically 30-60 days). The bank will investigate and provisionally credit the amount. Also consider filing a cybercrime complaint.
rejected: Contact the merchant directly. The bank cannot help you with this.

prompt: How do I choose between a fixed and floating interest rate for a home loan?
chosen: Choose a fixed rate if you prefer payment stability and expect interest rates to rise in the future. Choose a floating rate if you expect rates to fall or want to benefit from RBI rate cuts, as floating-rate loans often have no prepayment charges. Currently, floating rates are typically lower than fixed rates. Evaluate your risk tolerance and market outlook before deciding.
rejected: Always choose a fixed rate. Floating rates are risky and always increase.

prompt: What is the tax benefit of investing in PPF?
chosen: PPF offers triple tax benefit (EEE - Exempt-Exempt-Exempt): contributions up to Rs. 1.5 lakh qualify for deduction under Section 80C, the interest earned is fully tax-free, and the maturity amount is also exempt from tax. This makes PPF one of the most tax-efficient investment instruments available for long-term savings.
rejected: PPF has no tax benefits. All interest is taxable.

prompt: What is the difference between a debit card and a credit card?
chosen: A debit card is linked to your bank account and uses your existing funds when you spend. A credit card allows you to borrow money up to a preset limit, with repayment due by the monthly due date. Debit cards help you stay within budget since you can only spend what you have. Credit cards build credit history and offer rewards but can lead to debt if not managed carefully.
rejected: Debit and credit cards are identical. They both work the same way.

prompt: What is a good credit utilization ratio?
chosen: A credit utilization ratio below 30% is considered healthy and has a positive impact on your credit score. For example, if your total credit limit across all cards is Rs. 1 lakh, keep your total outstanding balance below Rs. 30,000. High utilization signals financial stress to lenders. Paying bills in full each month keeps utilization at 0%, which is ideal.
rejected: Use as much of your credit limit as possible. High utilization is fine.

prompt: How does a fixed deposit work?
chosen: In a fixed deposit, you deposit a lump sum with the bank for a fixed tenure (7 days to 10 years) at a predetermined interest rate. Interest is paid either periodically (monthly/quarterly) or compounded and paid at maturity. The principal and interest are guaranteed. Premature withdrawal is allowed but attracts a penalty of 0.5-1% on the applicable interest rate.
rejected: A fixed deposit is the same as a savings account with a different name.

prompt: What is the safest way to pay online?
chosen: Use your bank's verified payment page or UPI apps from official app stores. Never save card details on unsecured websites. Use virtual card numbers for one-time online purchases if available. Enable transaction alerts to monitor activity in real time. Use two-factor authentication and ensure the payment page URL begins with HTTPS. Avoid making payments on public computers.
rejected: Save your card details everywhere for convenience. It's always safe.

prompt: Can I take a loan against my mutual fund investments?
chosen: Yes, you can take a loan against mutual funds by pledging your units as collateral. The loan amount is typically 50-80% of the fund's NAV, depending on fund type. Interest rates are lower than personal loans. The units remain invested and continue to earn returns. This is a cost-effective way to meet short-term liquidity needs without redeeming investments.
rejected: You cannot take any loans against mutual fund investments.

prompt: What is the maximum amount I can transfer using UPI in a day?
chosen: The daily UPI transaction limit varies by bank but is generally Rs. 1 lakh per day, per UPI ID. Some banks offer higher limits for verified or premium account holders. NPCI sets an overall cap of Rs. 1 lakh per transaction for most UPI apps. For higher amounts, use NEFT or RTGS. Check your specific bank's UPI limits in the mobile banking app.
rejected: UPI has no transaction limits. You can transfer any amount.

prompt: What is the benefit of making prepayments on a loan?
chosen: Prepaying a loan reduces the outstanding principal, which directly reduces the total interest you pay over the loan tenure. Even a small lump-sum prepayment can save a significant amount in interest, especially in the early years. For floating-rate loans, prepayment is typically free. Prepayment accelerates your path to becoming debt-free and improves your net worth.
rejected: Prepaying a loan has no benefit. Just pay your regular EMI.

prompt: What are the risks of investing in equity mutual funds?
chosen: Equity mutual funds carry market risk, meaning returns fluctuate based on stock market performance. You may receive less than your invested amount in the short term during market downturns. However, over long periods (5-10+ years), equity funds historically generate higher inflation-adjusted returns than other instruments. Invest only surplus funds you won't need in the near term.
rejected: Equity mutual funds are completely risk-free and always give high returns.

prompt: How do I report a banking fraud to the bank?
chosen: Report banking fraud immediately to your bank's 24/7 customer care helpline. Block affected accounts and cards. File an online complaint on the bank's website or mobile app. Also report to the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal at cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930 (cybercrime helpline). Document all transaction details and communications. Quick reporting maximizes the chances of fund recovery.
rejected: You cannot report fraud to the bank. They will not help you.

prompt: What is the tenure for which I can open a fixed deposit?
chosen: Fixed deposits can be opened for tenures ranging from 7 days to 10 years. Tax-saving FDs have a mandatory lock-in of 5 years. Interest rates generally increase with tenure up to a certain point. You can choose a tenure based on your liquidity needs and financial goals. At maturity, you can renew the FD, transfer the amount to your savings account, or reinvest.
rejected: Fixed deposits are only available for exactly 1 year.

prompt: What happens to the loan if the borrower passes away?
chosen: If a borrower passes away, the legal heirs or co-applicants become responsible for the outstanding loan. For secured loans (home, auto), the bank may sell the collateral to recover the outstanding amount. Life insurance or loan protection insurance policies can cover the outstanding loan, protecting the family from debt burden. This is why loan protection insurance is strongly recommended.
rejected: The loan is automatically cancelled when the borrower passes away.

prompt: What is an NBFC?
chosen: An NBFC (Non-Banking Financial Company) is a company registered under the Companies Act that provides banking-like services such as loans, investments, and leasing without holding a banking license. NBFCs cannot accept demand deposits or issue cheques. They are regulated by RBI and serve segments underserved by traditional banks. Examples include Bajaj Finance, HDFC Ltd., and Muthoot Finance.
rejected: NBFC and bank are exactly the same thing with no differences.

prompt: How do I check my bank account balance?
chosen: You can check your balance through: the bank's mobile app or net banking portal, SMS banking by sending a text to the bank's SMS number, a missed call to the bank's balance enquiry number, visiting an ATM, or checking your passbook at the branch. SMS and missed call services are typically free for registered mobile numbers.
rejected: The only way to check your balance is to visit the bank branch.

prompt: Is there a penalty for breaking a fixed deposit early?
chosen: Yes, premature withdrawal of a fixed deposit attracts a penalty, typically 0.5% to 1% reduction on the applicable interest rate for the period the deposit was held. For example, if your FD earned 6% for 6 months but the 6-month rate is 5.5%, you receive 5.5% minus 0.5% penalty = 5%. Some banks waive the penalty on FDs broken to reinvest at a higher rate.
rejected: There is no penalty for breaking a fixed deposit at any time.

prompt: What is a sweep-in facility?
chosen: A sweep-in (auto-sweep) facility automatically transfers excess funds from your savings account into a fixed deposit when the balance exceeds a set threshold, and sweeps back funds when the savings account balance falls below a minimum. This earns higher FD interest on idle funds while maintaining liquidity. It is available on select savings account types.
rejected: A sweep-in facility is not available in banking.

prompt: What is the difference between NRE and NRO accounts?
chosen: An NRE (Non-Resident External) account holds foreign earnings converted to Indian rupees; the principal and interest are freely repatriable abroad, and interest is tax-free in India. An NRO (Non-Resident Ordinary) account holds Indian earnings (rent, pension, dividends); repatriation is restricted up to USD 1 million per year, and interest is taxable in India.
rejected: NRE and NRO accounts are the same and have no differences.

prompt: Can I use my credit card for international transactions?
chosen: Yes, international usage can be enabled on your credit card through the bank's mobile app or net banking. Transactions will be billed in INR at the prevailing foreign exchange rate plus a foreign transaction fee (typically 1.5-3.5%). Check for cards with zero forex markup for frequent international use. Inform the bank of international travel to avoid security blocks.
rejected: Credit cards only work in India and cannot be used abroad.

prompt: What is a loan moratorium?
chosen: A loan moratorium is a temporary period during which the borrower is not required to make loan repayments. It is typically offered at the beginning of a loan (3-6 months post-disbursement for education loans) or during financial crises as regulatory relief. Interest continues to accrue during the moratorium and may be added to the principal, potentially increasing the total repayment amount.
rejected: A moratorium means your loan is cancelled and you do not need to repay anything.

prompt: How is interest on a savings account calculated?
chosen: Savings account interest is calculated on the daily closing balance and credited to the account quarterly or monthly, depending on the bank. The formula is: Interest = (Daily Balance × Rate × Number of Days) / (365 × 100). Even small daily fluctuations in balance can affect the quarterly interest credit. Maintaining a higher balance consistently maximizes interest earned.
rejected: Interest on a savings account is calculated only on the minimum balance maintained in the month.

prompt: What is the Lock-in period for tax-saving fixed deposits?
chosen: Tax-saving fixed deposits have a mandatory lock-in period of 5 years. You cannot withdraw or take a loan against these FDs before maturity. Investments up to Rs. 1.5 lakh in tax-saving FDs qualify for deduction under Section 80C. The interest earned is taxable as per your income tax slab. Unlike regular FDs, these cannot be broken prematurely.
rejected: Tax-saving FDs have no lock-in period and can be withdrawn anytime.

prompt: What should I do if I forget my net banking password?
chosen: Click 'Forgot Password' on the bank's net banking login page. You will be asked to verify your identity using your account number, registered mobile number for OTP, and debit card details. After verification, you can create a new password. Alternatively, visit a branch with your ID proof. For security, use a strong, unique password and change it periodically.
rejected: You must visit the branch to reset your net banking password; it cannot be done online.

prompt: What is a bank's KYC update requirement?
chosen: Banks are required by RBI to periodically update KYC information for all account holders. Low-risk customers need to update KYC every 10 years, medium-risk every 8 years, and high-risk every 2 years. You will receive a notification from the bank. Failure to update KYC may result in transaction restrictions on your account. Submit updated identity and address proof to the bank.
rejected: KYC is a one-time process. You never need to update it again.

prompt: Is my bank deposit safe?
chosen: Bank deposits up to Rs. 5 lakh (principal + interest combined) per depositor per bank are insured by the Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) in India. This means even if a bank fails, your deposits up to this limit are protected. For higher amounts, diversify across banks or invest in government-backed instruments for safety.
rejected: Bank deposits are completely unprotected. If a bank fails, you lose all your money.

prompt: What is the difference between a demat account and a trading account?
chosen: A Demat account holds your securities (shares, bonds, ETFs) in electronic form. A trading account is used to buy and sell securities on the stock exchange. Both are needed to invest in stocks: you place orders through the trading account, and shares are stored or debited from your Demat account upon settlement. They are usually opened together with a broker.
rejected: A demat account and trading account are the same account.

prompt: What is loan processing fee?
chosen: A loan processing fee is a one-time charge levied by the bank for evaluating and processing your loan application. It typically ranges from 0.5% to 2% of the loan amount, plus GST at 18%. It is usually deducted from the loan disbursement. Processing fees are non-refundable even if the loan is rejected. Negotiate the fee and compare it across banks when applying.
rejected: Banks never charge any fees for processing loan applications.

prompt: What is a credit card statement?
chosen: A credit card statement is a monthly summary of all transactions made on your card during the billing cycle, including purchases, fees, interest charged, and payments made. It shows the total amount due, minimum amount due, and payment due date. Review your statement carefully each month to track spending, verify accuracy, and plan payments to avoid interest charges.
rejected: A credit card statement is just a receipt for your last purchase.

prompt: What is the eligibility criteria for a personal loan?
chosen: Eligibility for a personal loan typically requires: age between 21-60 years, minimum monthly income (usually Rs. 15,000-25,000 for salaried; varies for self-employed), employment stability (minimum 6-12 months with current employer), a good CIBIL score (usually 700+), and low existing debt obligations. Self-employed individuals also need minimum business vintage of 2-3 years.
rejected: Anyone can get a personal loan regardless of income or credit history.

prompt: Can I change my credit card billing cycle?
chosen: Some banks allow you to change your credit card billing cycle date by requesting it through the mobile app, net banking, or customer care. Changing the billing date can help align the payment due date with your salary credit, making it easier to pay on time. Not all banks offer this flexibility, so check with your card issuer about this option.
rejected: Once set, a credit card billing cycle can never be changed.

prompt: What is the benefit of using a forex card over cash for international travel?
chosen: A forex card is safer than cash because it can be blocked and replaced if lost or stolen. It offers locked-in exchange rates, protecting against currency fluctuations. Forex cards typically have lower transaction fees than using a regular debit or credit card abroad. Most cards allow you to load multiple currencies and reload them remotely if needed during travel.
rejected: Always carry foreign cash. Forex cards are unreliable and not accepted abroad.

prompt: How do I close a fixed deposit before maturity?
chosen: To break a fixed deposit prematurely, visit a bank branch or use net banking (if the facility is available online). Submit a premature closure request. The bank will calculate interest at the rate applicable for the actual period held, minus the premature withdrawal penalty (typically 0.5-1%). The proceeds will be credited to your linked savings account.
rejected: Fixed deposits cannot be closed before the maturity date under any circumstance.

prompt: What is the importance of maintaining financial records?
chosen: Maintaining financial records including bank statements, tax filings, loan documents, insurance policies, and investment certificates is important for loan applications, tax compliance, insurance claims, and financial planning. The Income Tax Department may require records going back 6-7 years. Digital storage and organized physical filing helps you access critical documents quickly when needed.
rejected: Financial records are not important. You don't need to keep them.
